title: Shrimp and Mushroom Linguine
ingredients: 1 pound large shrimp, peeled and deveined*
½ tsp. freshly ground black pepper
12 ounces linguine, cooked al dente and drained
instructions: In large skillet, melt butter over medium heat and sauté shrimp for 2-3 minutes or just until shrimp turn opaque (don’t overcook).  Remove shrimp and set aside.  Add mushrooms to skillet and sauté for 5 minutes.  Add tomatoes to skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes.  Season mushrooms and tomatoes with salt.  Slowly add cream and cook until sauce is reduced by 1/3 (about 5-7 minutes).  Stir in Parmesan and cook until melted and well combined, about 2-3 minutes.  Stir in basil, brandy flavoring and shrimp.  Cook for 1 minute until shrimp is warm.  Add hot, cooked and well-drained linguine to sauce.  Stir gently  for 1 minute until sauce is well combined with noodles.  Place in serving dish.  Sprinkle with additional grated Parmesan cheese if desired.    Serve immediately with crusty bread.  (Goes well with cooked peas.)
*If using frozen, cooked shrimp, defrost shrimp well.  Do not cook over butter, but just stir in at the end of the recipe to warm shrimp.
